2604.17159.title: Systematic Capability Benchmarking of Frontier Large Language Models for Offensive Cyber Tasks
2604.17159.paper_summary: We present, to our knowledge, the most comprehensive cross-model evaluation of LLM agents on offensive cybersecurity tasks, benchmarking 10 frontier models from 7 providers on all 200 challenges of the NYU CTF Bench. Building on the D-CIPHER multi-agent framework, we extend it with multi-provider backend support, a custom Kali Linux environment with over 100 pre-installed penetration testing tools, and runtime tool-discovery agents. Through a controlled factorial study, we find that the Kali Linux environment yields a +9.5 percentage-point improvement over Ubuntu, while auto-prompting and category-specific tips often degrade performance in well-equipped environments. Among models, Claude 4.5 Opus achieves the highest solve rate (59%), followed by Gemini 3 Pro (52%), with Gemini 3 Flash offering the best cost-efficiency at $0.05 per solve. Asymmetric planner/executor model assignments provide no meaningful benefit while coherent same-model configurations consistently outperform mixed-tier pairings. Our results indicate that environment tooling and model selection emerge as the strongest drivers of performance, whereas prompt engineering interventions show diminishing or negative returns in well-equipped environments. Reported performance reflects both model reasoning ability and compatibility with agent tooling and API integration.
2604.17159.paper_summary_zh: 我們呈現了截至目前為止，對於大型語言模型（LLM）代理在攻擊性網路安全任務上的最全面的跨模型評估，對來自7個提供者的10個前沿模型在NYU CTF Bench的所有200個挑戰進行基準測試。基於D-CIPHER多代理框架，我們擴展了其多提供者後端支持，並建立了一個自定義的Kali Linux環境，內含超過100個預裝的滲透測試工具，以及運行時工具發現代理。通過一項受控的因子研究，我們發現Kali Linux環境相比Ubuntu提高了9.5個百分點的性能，而自動提示和特定類別的提示在設備良好的環境中往往會降低性能。在模型中，Claude 4.5 Opus達到了最高的解題率（59%），其次是Gemini 3 Pro（52%），而Gemini 3 Flash則以每次解題0.05美元的成本效率最佳。非對稱規劃者/執行者模型分配並未帶來有意義的好處，而一致的同模型配置則持續超越混合層級的配對。我們的結果顯示，環境工具和模型選擇是性能的最強驅動因素，而提示工程干預在設備良好的環境中顯示出逐漸減少或負面的回報。報告的性能反映了模型的推理能力以及與代理工具和API集成的兼容性。
2604.17159.author: Tyler H. Merves et.al.
2604.17159.authors: Tyler H. Merves, Michael H. Conaway, Joseph M. Escobar, Hakan T. Otal, Unal Tatar
